retrieved_docs.title: ACC/AHA Guidelines for Valve Disease -- Summary / Explanation -- Prosthetic Valves
retrieved_docs.content: For patients with symptomatic prosthetic valve stenosis, repeat surgical or transcatheter intervention is indicated depending on surgical risk. For patients with paravalvular regurgitation or severe valvular regurgitation causing intractable hemolysis or NYHA Class III or IV heart failure symptoms, surgical reintervention is recommended. For those with a high surgical risk and favorable anatomy, transcatheter paravalvular leak repair or a valve-in-valve procedure is reasonable.

retrieved_docs.title: ACC/AHA Guidelines for Valve Disease -- Summary / Explanation
retrieved_docs.content: In patients with stage D secondary primary MR, as well as select patients with secondary MR remaining severely symptomatic despite optimal medical therapy for heart failure, TEER can be considered for LVEF 20% to 50%, LVESV less than or equal to 70 mm, and PASP less than 70 mm Hg and are at high or prohibitive risk for surgery. TEE is indicated to determine the procedure's suitability. Mitral valve surgery can be considered in stage D secondary MR when LVEF is greater than or equal to 50% for those with severe persistent symptoms as well as those with chronic severe secondary MR related to LV systolic dysfunction (LVEF less than 50%) while on optimal guideline-directed medical therapy for heart failure.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 3 -- Community 1936
retrieved_docs.content: Elevated mean arterial pressure, hypertensive crisis (أزمة فرط ضغط الدم), primary pulmonary hypertension (ارتفاع ضغط الدم الرِئَوِيّ الأَوَّلِيّ), and reactive hypertension (فرط ضغط الدم الانتيابي) are all classified within the broader vascular category. These conditions represent distinct manifestations of abnormal vascular pressure regulation. Elevated mean arterial pressure reflects a sustained increase in the average arterial pressure throughout the cardiac cycle. Hypertensive crisis denotes an acute, severe elevation of blood pressure that can threaten organ function if not promptly treated. Primary pulmonary hypertension is a specific form of high pressure that originates in the pulmonary arteries, leading to right‑ventricular strain and potential respiratory complications. Reactive hypertension describes a secondary rise in systemic blood pressure that occurs in response to another underlying condition. Recognizing that these entities share a common vascular basis aids clinicians in assessing, differentiating, and managing patients with hypertension‑related disorders, thereby improving diagnostic accuracy and therapeutic outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 4 -- Community 2895
retrieved_docs.content: Elevated mean arterial pressure is classified as a vascular condition, and it belongs to the broader Vascular category that also encompasses أزمة فرط ضغط الدم (hypertensive crisis), فرط ضغط الدم الانتيابي (diastolic hypertension), and فرط ضغط الدم الانقباضي (systolic hypertension). These entities represent related subtypes of abnormal blood pressure regulation. Elevated mean arterial pressure reflects a persistently higher average arterial pressure, while hypertensive crisis denotes an acute, severe elevation that can threaten organ function. Diastolic hypertension is characterized by consistently high diastolic pressure, and systolic hypertension is defined by consistently high systolic pressure. Recognizing that all of these disorders fall under the Vascular umbrella aids clinicians in categorizing blood‑pressure abnormalities, tailoring diagnostic evaluation, and selecting appropriate therapeutic strategies to improve patient outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 2291
retrieved_docs.content: HEART: GENERAL AND MISCELLANEOUS OPERATIVE PROCEDURES is a broad surgical category that encompasses several specific cardiac interventions. Among its subprocedures are Temporary cardioassist with artificial heart, which involves the use of a mechanical device to provide short‑term circulatory support when the native heart cannot maintain adequate output, and Operation on heart and pericardium, which includes surgical techniques directed at repairing or modifying the myocardium, valves, coronary vessels, or the surrounding pericardial sac. Together, these child procedures illustrate the range of operative options available for managing acute cardiac failure, structural heart disease, and pericardial pathology within the overarching framework of general and miscellaneous heart surgery.
